prs.title: [ROCm] ROCm DeepEP API updated to latest
prs.author: itej89
prs.body_head:
Enablement of all the DeepEP API parameters

## Purpose
This PR is to match ROCm DeepEP API with the vllm 

co-authored by : @lcskrishna 

The following changes are preformed

Code
- DeepEP buffer initialization arguments
- DeepEP dispatch call arguments support for low latency

ROCm Docker test stage
- Updates to the DeepEP and ROCShmem Commit IDs 
- Installation of rdma-core v62 required for latest ROCShmem

## Test Plan
**STEP1:** Build ROCm VLLM Image
```
docker build -f ./docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base -t  <IMG_VLLM_ROCM_BASE>  . 

DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build --no-cache  --target test --progress=plain -t  <FINAL_VLLM_IMAGE_NAME>  -f ./docker/Dockerfile.rocm  --build-
arg BASE_IMAGE=<IMG_VLLM_ROCM_BASE>   .

```
**STEP 2:** Please use below command to run VLLM with DeepEP on single node. Select _--all2all-backend_  as either  _deepep_low_latency_ or _deepep_high_throughput_.
```
docker run -it --rm \
  --device /dev/dri \
  --device /dev/kfd \
  --privileged \
  --device /dev/infiniband \
  -v /dev/infiniband:/dev/infiniband \
  --network host \
  --ipc host \
  --group-add video \
  --cap-add SYS_PTRACE \
  --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
  --ulimit memlock=-1 \
  --shm-size 64G \
  -v <HOST_DS3_MODEL_PATH>:/models/DeepSeek-V3\
  -e HSA_NO_SCRATCH_RECLAIM=1 \
  -e ROCSHMEM_HEAP_SIZE=7524589824 \
  -e VLLM_ENGINE_READY_TIMEOUT_S=7800 \
  --name <CONTAINER_NAME> \
  --entrypoint vllm \
 <FINAL_VLLM_IMAGE_NAME> \
  serve /models/DeepSeek-V3 \
    --tensor-parallel-size 1 \
    --data-parallel-size 8 \
    --enforce-eager \
    --enable-expert-parallel \
    --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8 \
    --host 127.0.0.1 \
    --port 2000 \
    --all2all-backend   deepep_low_latency | deepep_high_throughput
```
**STEP 3:** Please use below command to run VLLM with DeepEP on two nodes. Select _--all2all-backend_  as either  _deepep_low_latency_ or _deepep_high_throughput_.
```
# On Node 1
docker run -it --rm \

prs.title: [Bugfix][Rocm]Aiter MoE re-uses existing tensor addresses after weight update.
prs.author: yuankaichen-amd
prs.body_head:
The AMD aiter FusedMoE requires model weights be shuffled before use. When using this feature in RL scenario, however, the MoE weights will be re-shuffled time and again whenever a weight update is carried out.

The issue is that the weight shuffle should NOT change the tensor addresses -- otherwise captured CUDA graph will not be able to use the new weights.

The fix in this PR is to preserve the weights' addresses if they already exist.

## Purpose
Aiter MoE re-uses existing tensor addresses after weight update.

## Test Plan
Test with "export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_MOE=1" in RL setup (e.g. veRL + vLLM, enforce_eager=False).

## Test Result
Without the fix, the generated responses are all gibberish. The fix restores the correctness.

---
<details>
<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

- [ ] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
- [ ] The test plan, such as providing test command.
- [ ] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results
- [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.
</details>

prs.title: [ROCm] Use unified decode fallback for sliding-window AITER FA
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
## Summary

The decode path in `rocm_aiter_fa.py` already documents that sliding-window attention should bypass `paged_attention_v1` and use the unified attention fallback, but the condition only checked small head sizes. This patch moves that rule into a small helper, applies it in the decode branch, and adds a narrow regression test that locks the sliding-window case in.

## Why this isn't duplicate work

- Checked nearby ROCm issue and PR activity for the same area, including `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "sliding window unified_attention ROCM_AITER_FA"`
- Checked adjacent `rocm_aiter_fa` lanes such as `#33761`, `#39192`, and `#39527`
- The open work I found in this file is about KV-cache layout or broader kernel changes; I did not find an open PR that covers this decode fallback condition.

## Test Plan

- `wsl -e bash --noprofile --norc -lc 'cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-aiter-fa-sliding-window-fallback && .venv/bin/python -m pytest --noconftest tests/v1/attention/test_rocm_aiter_fa_utils.py -q'`
- `wsl -e bash --noprofile --norc -lc 'cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-aiter-fa-sliding-window-fallback && .venv/bin/python -m py_compile vllm/v1/attention/backends/rocm_aiter_fa.py vllm/v1/attention/backends/rocm_aiter_fa_utils.py tests/v1/attention/test_rocm_aiter_fa_utils.py'`
- `git diff --check`

## Test Result

- `tests/v1/attention/test_rocm_aiter_fa_utils.py`: `2 passed`
- `py_compile` passed for the touched code and test files
- `git diff --check` passed

## AI assistance

I used AI assistance to help draft and validate the patch, and I reviewed the diff and command output before opening this PR.

prs.title: [ROCm] Fix AssertionError in ActivationQuantFusionPass when torch.compile is used on ROCm
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
PR #38817 changed the `SiluMulBlockQuantPattern` registration guard from `is_cuda()` to `is_cuda_alike()`, inadvertently enabling it on ROCm. However, this pattern requires `per_token_group_fp8_quant` in `QUANT_OPS` (from `rms_quant_fusion.py`), and that op is CUDA-only (`#ifndef USE_ROCM` in `csrc/libtorch_stable/torch_bindings.cpp`).

On ROCm, `QUANT_OPS` does not contain `kFp8Dynamic128Sym` or `kFp8Dynamic64Sym`, so `ActivationQuantFusionPass.__init__` crashes when torch.compile is used:
```
AssertionError: unsupported quantization scheme kFp8Dynamic128Sym
```

The pattern can never match on ROCm anyway since the unfused `per_token_group_fp8_quant` op does not exist there. ROCm uses `rocm_aiter_fusion.py` for its own block-quant fusions.

Fix: revert the `SiluMulBlockQuantPattern` loop guard to `is_cuda()`. The `FUSED_OPS` entry for `silu_and_mul_per_block_quant` remains `is_cuda_alike()` for potential future use.

**Duplicate check:** No existing open PR addresses this. This is a regression introduced in #38817 (merged Apr 8, 2026).

**Tests:** `ActivationQuantFusionPass` is only instantiated during `torch.compile`; verified the guard logic by tracing `QUANT_OPS` population in `rms_quant_fusion.py` (lines 82-84, CUDA-only) against the `is_cuda_alike()` check.

**AI assistance:** Authored with Claude Code assistance.

cc @jeffdaily @sunway513 @jithunnair-amd @pruthvistony @ROCmSupport @gshtras @hongxiayang @naromero77amd

prs.title: [ROCm][CI/Build] Guard gfx115x Docker base compatibility
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
## Summary
- add a lightweight ROCm Docker config validator for `docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base`
- fail if `gfx1150` / `gfx1151` are advertised with a pre-`7.2` ROCm base image
- wire the check into pre-commit and cover the compatibility matrix with a focused unit test

## Why this is not duplicating an existing PR
Issue `#31333` reported that `docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base` was still on ROCm `7.0`. Current `main` is already on `rocm/dev-ubuntu-22.04:7.2.1-complete`, so this PR does **not** try to re-land that base-image bump.

Instead, this is a follow-up guard to keep that regression from coming back: if `PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH` includes `gfx1150` / `gfx1151`, the new check rejects an older ROCm base image before it ships.

I also checked for an open PR tied to `31333` / this exact guard and did not find one.

## Testing
- `uv run --no-project --with pytest python -m pytest --noconftest tests/test_check_rocm_docker_config.py -q`
- `uv run --no-project tools/pre_commit/check_rocm_docker_config.py`
- `uv run --no-project --with pre-commit pre-commit run check-forbidden-imports --files tools/pre_commit/check_rocm_docker_config.py tests/test_check_rocm_docker_config.py`
- `uv run --no-project --with pre-commit pre-commit run check-rocm-docker-config --files docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base`
- `uv run --no-project --with ruff ruff check tools/pre_commit/check_rocm_docker_config.py tests/test_check_rocm_docker_config.py`
- `uv run --no-project --with ruff ruff format --check tools/pre_commit/check_rocm_docker_config.py tests/test_check_rocm_docker_config.py`

Note: I also tried a broader `pre-commit run --files ...` pass on the changed files, but on this Windows shell it hit an unrelated existing `/bin/bash` availability problem in the repo's `update-dockerfile-graph` hook. I am not claiming that broad hook pass is green here.

AI assistance was used for code search, issue/PR triage, and drafting the guard/test implementation; all changes were reviewed by the human submitter.

prs.title: [ROCm][Bugfix] Forward router-weight flag in Quark OCP MX AITER MoE
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
Refs #40008.

This keeps the Quark OCP MX AITER apply path from silently falling back at runtime after weights may have been prepared for AITER/CK, and forwards `apply_router_weight_on_input` into `rocm_aiter_fused_experts` so routing-weight-on-input models preserve the same semantics as the modular and fused fallback paths.

Why this is not duplicating an existing PR:
- `gh issue view 40008 --repo vllm-project/vllm --comments`
- `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "40008 in:body"` returned no matches when this PR was opened.
- `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "Unsupported kernel config for moe heuristic dispatch"` returned no matches when this PR was opened.
- `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "moe heuristic dispatch rocm"` only found #38665, which is a different ROCm lane.

Tests run:
- `wsl.exe --cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-moe-heuristic-40008 bash -lc 'PYTHONPATH=$PWD uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with torch==2.11.0 --with pytest --with-requirements requirements/common.txt --with-requirements requirements/test/cuda.in python -m pytest tests/model_executor/layers/test_quark_ocp_mx_moe.py -q'` -> `2 passed`
- `wsl.exe --cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-moe-heuristic-40008 bash -lc 'uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with ruff ruff check vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/quark/quark_moe.py tests/model_executor/layers/test_quark_ocp_mx_moe.py'` -> `All checks passed!`
- `wsl.exe --cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-moe-heuristic-40008 bash -lc 'uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with ruff ruff format --check vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/quark/quark_moe.py tests/model_executor/layers/test_quark_ocp_mx_moe.py'` -> `2 files already formatted`
- `git diff --check` -> passed

AI assistance: I used Codex to help draft the patch and regression tests, then reviewed the diff

prs.title: [Quant] Consolidate GPTQ: rename gptq_marlin.py to auto_gptq.py
prs.author: chengyinie
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Consolidate GPTQ quantization by renaming `gptq_marlin.py` to `auto_gptq.py` as requested in #37765.
**Key changes:**
- Renames `gptq_marlin.py` → `auto_gptq.py`
- `GPTQMarlinConfig.get_name()` now returns `"auto_gptq"`
- Adds `override_quantization_method()` to auto-convert models with `quant_method: gptq` to use `auto_gptq`
- Updates all imports across the codebase to use `auto_gptq` module
- Adds `auto_gptq` to overrides list in `model.py`
- Adds `test_auto_gptq.py` for the new quantization method
- Updates `test_gptq_marlin.py` skipif condition to check `auto_gptq` support
- Maintains backward compatibility: `quantization="gptq"` still works (maps to GPTQMarlinConfig)
Closes #37765

## Test Plan
```bash
pytest tests/quantization/test_auto_gptq.py -v -s
pytest tests/quantization/test_gptq_dynamic.py -v -s
pytest tests/quantization/test_lm_head.py -v -s
pytest tests/models/quantization/test_gptq_marlin.py -v -s

## Test Result
 All passed.
---

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Build fastsafetensors from source so it links against libamdhip64
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
The `fastsafetensors` wheel on PyPI is CUDA-only. Its compiled extension `dlopen`s `libcudart.so` unconditionally, so on ROCm hosts it throws:

```
Exception: [FAIL] GPU runtime library (libcudart.so or libamdhip64.so) does not exist
```

even though `libamdhip64.so` is present. This breaks both the ROCm CI test image and the ROCm release image today. Installing it directly from the pinned git tag forces build from source. Resolves failure in `mi250_1: Model Executor` test group:

```bash
pytest tests/model_executor/model_loader/fastsafetensors_loader/
```

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm] Add env flags to disable dynamic MXFP4 quant and enable AITER tuned GEMMs for Attention Projection Layers
prs.author: heachary
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
For models in the DeepSeek family, dynamic MXFP4 quantization is enabled by default for the attention projection layers. This behavior may be suboptimal in certain deployment scenarios.

This PR introduces two opt-in environment variables that provide finer control over this path:

1. The ability to disable dynamic MXFP4 quantization for attention projections.
2. The ability to route unquantized attention GEMMs through AITER's newer FlyDSL-based tuned GEMM implementations, instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

In internal benchmarks, using the FlyDSL GEMM path for unquantized attention projections shows a good performance improvement on models such as Kimi-K2-Thinking.

## Summary
This PR adds two new ROCm environment variables to give operators finer control over quantization and GEMM dispatch for attention linear layers:
- **`VLLM_ROCM_DISABLE_ATTENTION_LINEAR_LAYER_DYNAMIC_MXFP4_QUANT`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, skips the dynamic MXFP4 quantization that is applied to attention projection layers (`q_proj`, `k_proj`, `v_proj`, `o_proj`) which is done upon model load for a certain family of models.
- **`VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_TUNED_UNQUANTISED_GEMM`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, routes unquantized GEMMs in the attention path through AITER's tuned GEMM implementation (`aiter.tuned_gemm.tgemm.mm`) instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

Both flags are opt-in and default to the current behavior, so there is no change for existing users.

## Test Result
### Accuracy
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

Benchmark | Metric | Score | Threshold | Status
-- | -- | -- | -- | --
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (flexible-extract) | 0.944 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (strict-match) | 0.936 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS

### Performance
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

| config | conc | Baseline | AiterTunedFI |

prs.title: [Bugfix][CI][Hardware][AMD] Fix various e4m3fn -> e4m3fnuz normalization issues
prs.author: mawong-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
This PR fixes various issues in e4m3fnuz handling encountered by various quantization code paths (ModelOpt, online). In particular, on `e4m3fnuz` platforms, `normalize_e4m3fn_to_e4m3fnuz` should be called exactly once on a given FP8 linear layer after weights are loaded.
However,
- Some PyTorch linear kernel-based code paths used during ModelOpt quantization were not calling this function.
- Some code paths used in online quantization methods were trying to call this function twice, once in the linear layer and once more in the linear layer's kernel implementation.

The end result is that the Quantization test group should now be passing on ROCm.

This PR also removes a Quark mxfp4 model that was causing failures in the Kernels MoE test group because it is incompatible with HF Transformers v5.
## Test Plan
`VLLM_TEST_FORCE_LOAD_FORMAT=auto pytest -v -s quantization/ --ignore quantization/test_blackwell_moe.py`
`pytest -v -s kernels/moe --ignore=kernels/moe/test_modular_oai_triton_moe.py`
These tests should be run as part of the Quantization and Kernels MoE test groups in vLLM AMD CI.

## Test Result
The above test groups pass, and there are no regressions.

cc @kenroche and @AndreasKaratzas

---

prs.title: [ROCm] Use quant_dtype in per_token_quant instead of hardcoded FP8
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
`_rocm_aiter_per_token_quant_impl` and its fake both accept `quant_dtype` (which can be `torch.int8` or `FP8_DTYPE` per the assert), but hardcode `FP8_DTYPE` for the output tensor allocation. This means passing `torch.int8` would still get an FP8 tensor back.

Simple fix: use `quant_dtype` instead of `FP8_DTYPE` for `torch.empty()`.

Currently all callers pass `FP8_DTYPE` so this hasn't surfaced, but it's a correctness bug if int8 quantization is ever enabled on this path.

No duplicate PRs found (PR #36092 covers other _aiter_ops bugs but not this one). AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: Revert "[MoE Refactor] Mxfp4 oracle rebased" (#37128)
prs.author: zhewenl
prs.body_head:
